text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
AND
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 56-year-old female with metastatic melanoma (BRAF V600E-mutated, Stage IV M1c), currently on nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks (maintenance monotherapy, cycle 17). History of prior disease involving bilateral axillary and cervical lymph nodes, hepatic parenchyma, peritoneum, and subcutaneous/cutaneous nodules. Prior treatment with dabrafenib/trametinib (progressed at 8 months), followed by ipilimumab/nivolumab combination (3 cycles, complicated by Grade 3 immune colitis, ipilimumab discontinued), then transitioned to nivolumab monotherapy maintenance. Most recent imaging approximately 6 months prior showed continued favorable response. Comprehensive restaging requested.

TECHNIQUE:
CT — Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed following administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast material. Images acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformatted images reviewed. Slice thickness: 5 mm axial with 2.5 mm reformats through the liver and upper abdomen.

MRI Brain — Multiplanar MRI of the brain performed at 3.0 Tesla with and without gadolinium (Gadavist 0.1 mmol/kg IV). Sequences: T1 pre/post, T2 FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging, gradient echo (susceptibility-weighted), 3D MPRAGE post-contrast. No motion degradation. Adequate study.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK (CERVICAL SOFT TISSUES, INCLUDED ON SCOUT AND UPPER CUTS):
The previously enlarged right level II cervical lymph node has markedly decreased in size and now measures approximately 0.3 cm in short-axis diameter. This node is no longer meaningfully measurable and falls below any size threshold for pathologic significance. No other cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy identified on the limited views included.

CHEST:
Lungs: The lung parenchyma appears clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation. No ground-glass opacities to suggest immune-related pneumonitis. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is not widened. No anterior mediastinal, paratracheal, subcarinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. Heart size is within normal limits. Pericardium is unremarkable.

Axillae: The right axillary region demonstrates a small residual lymph node measuring 0.5 cm in short-axis diameter, decreased from 0.6 cm on the prior examination. This node has been progressively diminishing over serial imaging. The left axilla is unremarkable with no enlarged nodes visualized.

Chest wall and pleura: No chest wall masses or abnormal soft tissue deposits. Pleural surfaces smooth and unremarkable bilaterally.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour. There is one residual hypodense focus in the right hepatic lobe (posterior segment, corresponding to segment 6 region on anatomic localization), now measuring approximately 0.3 cm in greatest dimension, slightly decreased from 0.4 cm on the prior CT. This lesion continues to diminish in size. The second hepatic lesion that measured 0.4 cm on the immediately prior examination is no longer confidently identified — this could reflect true resolution, or the lesion may fall below the current limit of CT resolution. No new hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable without focal lesions or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: Normal bilaterally. No adrenal nodules or masses.

Kidneys: Normal bilateral renal enhancement without hydronephrosis, calculi, or suspicious solid masses. No perinephric stranding.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The bowel loops appear normal in caliber and wall thickness throughout. No bowel wall thickening to suggest active colitis — the previously noted diffuse colonic wall thickening from resolving immune-mediated colitis has completely resolved, with the colon appearing normal in appearance on this examination. No ascites. No free air.

Lymph Nodes (Abdomen/Retroperitoneum): No enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymph nodes identified. No periportal or celiac axis adenopathy.

PERITONEUM:
No peritoneal nodularity, omental caking, or free fluid identified. The previously described peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament, which was no longer visualized on prior imaging, remains absent on this examination. No recurrence of peritoneal disease.

PELVIS:
The uterus and adnexa are unremarkable. No pelvic sidewall or inguinal lymphadenopathy. No pelvic masses or free fluid.

SOFT TISSUES AND OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:
No abnormal subcutaneous or cutaneous nodules identified on the included imaging. No suspicious osseous lesions. No cortical destruction or marrow replacement. The visualized spine demonstrates mild degenerative changes, age-appropriate.

---

MRI BRAIN FINDINGS:

Brain Parenchyma: The cerebral and cerebellar hemispheres are symmetric and normal in signal characteristics. No focal areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarct. No areas of T2/FLAIR signal abnormality to suggest edema, vasogenic or cytotoxic, in either cerebral or cerebellar parenchyma.

Post-Contrast Enhancement: Following gadolinium administration, there is no abnormal parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or dural enhancement. No ring-enhancing or nodular enhancing lesions. No sulcal enhancement pattern to suggest leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.

Gradient Echo/SWI: No microhemorrhages, hemosiderin deposits, or susceptibility artifact consistent with prior hemorrhagic metastases. No blood products.

Ventricles and CSF Spaces: Ventricles are normal in size and configuration. Sulci are appropriate for stated age. No midline shift. No evidence of hydrocephalus.

Skull and Calvarium: No abnormal calvarial signal or enhancement. No lytic or blastic osseous lesions involving the calvarium.

Orbit and Skull Base: Unremarkable. Orbits symmetric. No retro-orbital mass. Of note, the patient has a documented history of prior anterior uveitis; globes appear normal on current imaging with no orbital abnormality.

Posterior Fossa: Cerebellum and brainstem are normal in morphology and signal. Cerebellar hemispheres symmetric. Fourth ventricle in normal position.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued and deepening favorable response in all previously involved sites compared with prior imaging and with baseline at start of immunotherapy.

2. Right axillary lymph node now diminished to 0.5 cm — markedly reduced from 4.2 cm at initial staging and continuing to shrink on serial studies.

3. Hepatic disease: one residual tiny hypodense focus in the right hepatic lobe measuring 0.3 cm, down from 0.4 cm previously. The second hepatic lesion previously noted at 0.4 cm is no longer visualized. This remaining focus may represent scar/fibrosis versus a very small residual viable deposit — morphologically indeterminate at this size by CT alone.

4. Right cervical lymph node no longer meaningfully measurable at approximately 0.3 cm. Peritoneal disease remains absent.

5. No new sites of disease identified anywhere in the body.

6. No intracranial metastases on MRI brain with gadolinium. No leptomeningeal disease.

7. Colonic wall appearance now normal, with complete resolution of prior inflammatory changes from immune-mediated colitis.

8. No imaging features to suggest immune-related pneumonitis or other organ toxicity.

Overall, imaging findings reflect a very deep, sustained treatment response across all previously involved disease sites, with only a tiny residual hepatic focus of uncertain significance remaining.
